inclusions.description: Unwrought zinc. Subheading Note definitions: (a) Zinc, not alloyed = metal ≥97.5% zinc by weight; (b) Zinc alloys = metallic substances where zinc predominates by weight over each other element AND total other elements >2.5% by weight; (c) Zinc dust = dust from condensation of zinc vapour, spherical particles with ≥80% by weight passing 63µm sieve, containing ≥85% metallic zinc by weight
Zinc dust, powders and flakes (Subheading Note 1(c) defines zinc dust specifically by particle size and zinc purity)
Zinc bars, rods, profiles and wire
Zinc plates, sheets, strip and foil
Zinc tubes, pipes and tube or pipe fittings (e.g., couplings, elbows, sleeves)
Other articles of zinc: gutters, roof capping, skylight frames, zinc-based die castings, zinc anodes, zinc alloy wheels, monuments/ornaments of zinc

exclusions.condition: Zinc compounds (zinc oxide, zinc sulphate, zinc chloride, zinc phosphate, etc.)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 28 (inorganic zinc compounds) or Chapter 29 (organic zinc compounds). Metallic zinc → Ch.79; chemical compounds of zinc → Ch.28/29

exclusions.condition: Zinc-coated (galvanised) steel or other base metals — zinc applied as coating/plating
exclusions.action: Redirect to the base metal substrate chapter: galvanised steel → Ch.72 (heading 72.10 or 72.14 etc.), galvanised copper → Ch.74. The zinc coating is incidental; the article is classified by the substrate metal

classification_rules.rule: Subheading Note 1 — Three definitions for zinc subheading classification
classification_rules.description: (a) Zinc NOT alloyed: ≥97.5% zinc by weight. (b) Zinc alloys: zinc predominates over each other element by weight AND total other elements >2.5% by weight (e.g., zinc-aluminium, zinc-copper, zinc-lead alloys used for die casting). (c) Zinc dust: obtained by CONDENSATION of zinc vapour, spherical particles, ≥80% by weight pass through 63µm sieve AND ≥85% metallic zinc content — distinguished from zinc powder (mechanically ground, typically larger and irregular particles). These definitions drive subheading splits within 79.01 and 79.03.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: Ch.79 (zinc metal) vs Ch.72 (galvanised steel) vs Ch.28 (zinc compounds)
classification_rules.description: Three paths for zinc-related goods: (1) Zinc as the primary structural metal → Ch.79. (2) Zinc as a protective COATING on another metal substrate → classified by substrate (galvanised steel → Ch.72, galvanised wire → 73.17, galvanised tube → 73.06). The zinc coating does not change the chapter. (3) Zinc in compound form (oxide, salt, etc.) → Ch.28 or Ch.29. Decision sequence: compound? → Ch.28/29. Zinc coating on another metal? → substrate chapter. Zinc as principal structural material? → Ch.79.

classification_rules.rule: Zinc dust (79.03) vs zinc powder (79.03) vs zinc flakes (79.03) — all in same heading but defined differently
classification_rules.description: Heading 79.03 covers three forms: (1) Zinc DUST = condensation product, spherical, fine (≥80% pass 63µm sieve, ≥85% metallic Zn) — used as reducing agent, anti-corrosion pigment. (2) Zinc POWDER = mechanically produced, coarser and less spherical than dust — used in batteries, chemical synthesis. (3) Zinc FLAKES = flat/lamellar particles, produced by cold rolling/milling — used in paints and coatings. All three classify in 79.03; the distinct definitions (Subheading Note 1(c)) matter for subheading determination of zinc dust vs. powder.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 79.07 (articles of zinc) vs Ch.83 (miscellaneous base metal articles) vs Ch.73 (iron/steel articles)
classification_rules.description: When a zinc article corresponds to a functional description in Ch.83 (e.g., zinc padlocks, zinc fittings), heading 79.07 competes with Ch.83. Ch.83 coveres articles 'of base metal' which includes zinc — so specific Ch.83 headings take priority over the residual 79.07. Zinc padlocks → 83.01. Zinc hardware/fittings for doors/windows → 83.02. Zinc gutters and roofing elements (no more specific heading) → 79.07. Die-cast zinc car parts → typically Ch.87 (vehicle parts). General zinc castings with no specific heading → 79.07.
